meta.description:
DCB tag plumbing:
Events with a Sku or CouponCode
property are tagged at append
time via new Event<T>(evt).AddTag(...).
Initialization registers both via
RegisterTagType<Sku>() and
RegisterTagType<CouponCode>().

DCB inventory boundary:
AddItem reads StockLevel via
FetchForWritingByTags(sku).
SaveChangesAsync asserts no
other Sku-tagged event slipped
in between read and write.

DCB single-use boundary:
ApplyCoupon reads CouponUsage via
FetchForWritingByTags(code).
Only one cart can ever win the
claim. Same primitive as stock,
different shape of rule.

OpenCartByCustomer is an *inline
projection* (not DCB) — same
consistency property though:
CreateCart reads it on the write
path, so the previous commit is
visible to the next request.

Projection lifecycles:
• GetCart        — inline snapshot
• CartTimeline   — live replay
                   (no daemon, no doc)
• SalesByDay     — async, daemon polls
